title: verbascum thapsus subsp. crassifolium
intro: Verbascum thapsus subsp. crassifolium, also known as Mullein, is a biennial plant belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and wounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, offering potential health benefits like reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and promoting skin health. Additionally, Mullein's medicinal properties have been traditionally used to treat conditions like bronchitis, asthma, and eczema, making it a popular herb in natural medicine.
